sanskrit:
अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ता: शरीरिण:।
अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत।।
transliteration: antavanta ime dehā nityasyoktāḥ śarīriṇaḥ / anāśino ’prameyasya tasmād yudhyasva bhārata
literal_meaning: Perishable are these bodies of the eternal embodied one; the imperishable, immeasurable Self—therefore, fight, descendant of Bharata.
contextual_meaning: The body is temporary, the Self is eternal; identify with the unchanging essence and act without fear of loss.
psychological_state: Dissolving death-anxiety by shifting identity from body to trans-personal Self; existential re-framing that enables purposeful action.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon hesitates to operate on a loved one, paralyzed by the thought of losing them on the table; remembering the verse, she steadies her hands, focusing on the duty to heal rather than the outcome.
anchor_text: Remember the body dies, not the Self; act with courage.
